beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check on sourdough starter and water the houseplants.
beats.reasoning: Early riser ritual—Raj wakes before 7am most days. Saturday morning is perfect for tending the temperamental starter and plants before heading out.

beats.activity: Commute to Ballard Farmers Market on foot and bike or bus.
beats.reasoning: Saturday farmers market is a natural draw for someone who values fresh herbs and adventurous eating. Ballard is a short trip from South Lake Union. Overcast weather is ideal for walking/biking.

beats.activity: Browse and shop at the Ballard Farmers Market for produce, herbs, and maybe a pastry or coffee.
beats.reasoning: Early arrival means less crowd. Raj enjoys the farmers market for fresh ingredients for baking and cooking; this fits his adventurous eating style and sourdough hobby.

beats.activity: Commute back to South Lake Union on foot/bus.
beats.reasoning: Return home with market haul. Overcast weather makes the walk pleasant. Budget travel time for transit or leisurely walk.

beats.activity: Unload groceries, prep ingredients, shape and proof sourdough for the day.
beats.reasoning: Saturday morning is prime time for sourdough work. Raj will shape the loaf and get it ready for afternoon baking while the starter is active.

beats.activity: Commute to T-Mobile Park to catch the Mariners home game at 1pm; arrive early for lunch and atmosphere.
beats.reasoning: Saturday 1pm Mariners game is a natural social/leisure opportunity. Raj is an early riser and planner; arriving early for lunch and the crowd energy fits his weekend mood. SoDo will be lively but manageable on an overcast day.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a vendor near T-Mobile Park and enjoy the Mariners game.
beats.reasoning: Adventurous eater will enjoy ballpark food variety. The 1pm start means lunch coincides with early innings; Raj can soak in the Saturday crowd energy without it being overwhelming.

beats.activity: Continue watching the Mariners game through mid-innings.
beats.reasoning: Typical baseball game length allows Raj to enjoy the Saturday social atmosphere while the overcast weather keeps the stadium comfortable. He'll leave before the end to head home for sourdough baking.

beats.activity: Commute home to South Lake Union.
beats.reasoning: Mid-game departure avoids the post-game crush and gets Raj home in time for final sourdough proofing and baking. Moderate travel time across town on a Saturday.

beats.activity: Score and bake the sourdough loaf; prepare a simple dinner with fresh herbs from the farmers market.
beats.reasoning: Core hobby and ritual. Timing the bake for late afternoon ensures the loaf finishes around dinner time. Fresh herbs from the morning market elevate the meal. Low energy day (45/100 yesterday) suggests staying home for restorative cooking.

beats.activity: Enjoy sourdough dinner with wine, maybe photograph the fresh loaf or organize film negatives.; Evening leisure: read, tidy up, prepare for sleep.
beats.reasoning: Quiet evening wind-down with food and creative pursuits (photography, darkroom work). Fits Raj's preference for close time and grounding rituals after a social day out.
6.5 hour sleep ending at 05:30, well before the 06:00 deadline. Raj is an early riser, so waking around 5:30am on Sunday is consistent with his habits.

world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
